title: Potamochloa retzii
taxonomy: Potamochloa retzii is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Potamochloa. The family Poaceae is commonly known as the grass family. The genus Potamochloa is a small group of aquatic grasses. Further classification of Potamochloa retzii is within the tribe of the family Poaceae.

intro: Potamochloa retzii, also known as rice grass or water grass, is a species of aquatic grass belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections, skin conditions, and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Potamochloa retzii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Potamochloa retzii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or topical paste, which may be used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, colds, and skin irritations.
